rephrased_problem: Reduce the time spent on manual data entry and report generation by at least 30% within 6 months, increase the number of tasks completed per employee by 15% in the same period, and provide leadership with a real‑time KPI dashboard covering operational throughput, error rates, and resource utilization.
clarified_assumptions: Manual work primarily consists of extracting data from documents, entering it into internal systems, and compiling periodic reports.
Productivity is measured by tasks completed per employee per hour and average processing time per task.
Visibility means leadership can view up‑to‑date operational metrics (throughput, error rate, resource usage) on a unified dashboard.
The organization already captures digital logs of tasks, system usage, and has a document repository.
There is a willingness to integrate a new AI component with existing workflow tools (e.g., ERP, ticketing system).
Data privacy and compliance requirements will be met by keeping all processing on‑premise or in a vetted cloud environment.
proposed_solution: Deploy an AI‑powered workflow automation platform that uses OCR and natural‑language processing to auto‑extract data from incoming documents, automatically populate enterprise systems, route tasks to the appropriate owners, and surface a real‑time KPI dashboard for leadership.
target_users: Operational staff who currently perform data entry and report compilation
Team leads who assign and monitor work
Executives and senior managers who need operational visibility
key_features: Document AI engine (OCR + NLP) for high‑accuracy data extraction
Rule‑based and AI‑driven task routing and auto‑population of ERP/CRM fields
Predictive workload balancing suggestions for team leads
Customizable real‑time KPI dashboard with drill‑down capability
Alerting system for anomalies (e.g., spikes in processing time or error rates)
Audit log for compliance and traceability
data_needed: Historical documents (invoices, forms, PDFs) and their manually entered outcomes
Task logs from the current workflow system (timestamps, assignee, status)
System logs from ERP/CRM showing field updates
Existing KPI definitions and historical performance metrics
User feedback on common pain points in current processes
success_metrics: Average manual processing time per document reduced by ≥30%
Data extraction accuracy ≥95% compared to manual entry
Tasks completed per employee per hour increased by ≥15%
Dashboard adoption rate ≥80% of leadership within 3 months of launch
Number of manual report generation steps reduced by ≥50%
implementation_steps: 1. Conduct detailed workshops with operations, IT, and leadership to finalize use cases and KPI definitions.
2. Gather and label a representative sample of documents and task logs for model training.
3. Build and validate the Document AI extraction model; iterate until accuracy targets are met.
4. Develop integration connectors to auto‑populate target systems and trigger routing rules.
5. Design and prototype the KPI dashboard; obtain stakeholder sign‑off.
6. Run a pilot with one business unit, monitor performance, and collect feedback.
7. Refine models, rules, and UI based on pilot results; prepare change‑management materials.
8. Roll out solution organization‑wide, provide training, and establish ongoing monitoring.
9. Establish a governance process for model retraining and KPI updates.
risks: Insufficient quality or volume of historical documents may limit model accuracy.
User resistance to changing established manual processes.
Integration challenges with legacy ERP/CRM systems.
Potential data privacy concerns if sensitive documents are processed in the cloud.
Over‑reliance on AI predictions without proper human oversight could lead to errors.

rephrased_requirement: Develop a secure, low‑latency AI‑powered feature that lets authenticated users upload files (e.g., PDF, DOCX, TXT), extracts the textual content, stores it safely, and enables users to ask natural‑language questions about the uploaded content. The system must return answers that are relevant and include a confidence indicator, while protecting user data and meeting performance targets.
functional_requirements: Provide a web/API endpoint for authenticated users to upload files up to a configurable size limit.
Validate uploaded files for allowed MIME types and scan for malware.
Extract text from supported file formats using OCR or parsing libraries.
Store extracted content and original files in encrypted storage.
Index the extracted content in a vector store for semantic retrieval.
Expose an endpoint where users can submit natural‑language questions referencing their uploaded files.
Perform retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG) using a large language model to produce answers.
Return the answer together with a confidence score and citation of source passages.
Log all upload and query activities for audit purposes.
Allow users to delete their uploaded files and associated data.
non_functional_requirements: Answer latency ≤ 2 seconds for queries on average (95th percentile ≤ 3 seconds).
Support at least 100 concurrent upload/query sessions with auto‑scaling.
Data retention configurable per user, with automatic purge after the defined period.
Observability: metrics, logs, and tracing for upload, extraction, indexing, and query pipelines.
security_requirements: All client‑to‑server communication must use TLS 1.2+.
Files and extracted text must be encrypted at rest (AES‑256).
Authentication via OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect; role‑based access control to restrict file access to owners only.
Virus/malware scanning of every uploaded file before processing.
Input validation and sanitization to prevent injection attacks.
Audit logging of user actions, file access, and model inference calls.
Compliance with GDPR/CCPA: ability to export or delete personal data on request.
acceptance_criteria: A user can successfully upload a PDF ≤ 20 MB and receive a 200 response.
Uploaded files are stored encrypted and are only accessible by the uploading user.
When a user asks a question about the uploaded document, the system returns an answer with a confidence score ≥ 0.8 in ≤ 2 seconds for 95% of queries.
All network traffic is captured as TLS‑encrypted; attempts to access another user's data are denied with 403.
Malicious files are detected and rejected with an appropriate error message.
Audit logs contain timestamp, user ID, operation type, and outcome for every upload and query.
System maintains ≥ 99.9% uptime over a 30‑day monitoring period.
recommended_solution_approach: Implement a microservice architecture using FastAPI (Python) behind an API gateway. Use Amazon S3 (or equivalent) with server‑side encryption for file storage and presigned URLs for uploads. Integrate ClamAV for malware scanning. Extract text with Apache Tika (for PDFs/DOCX) and Tesseract OCR for images. Store embeddings in a managed vector database such as Pinecone or OpenSearch k‑NN. Call a hosted LLM (e.g., OpenAI GPT‑4) via a RAG pattern: retrieve top‑k relevant passages, construct a prompt with citations, and request an answer. Return the answer with the model's logprob‑based confidence and source snippets. Deploy on a container orchestration platform (EKS/ECS or Kubernetes) with auto‑scaling, enable Prometheus/Grafana for observability, and enforce IAM policies for least‑privilege access. Use OAuth 2.0 for authentication and integrate with the existing identity provider. Implement CI/CD pipelines with automated security testing.
open_questions: Which specific file types and maximum file size should be supported?
What is the expected volume of uploads per day and peak concurrent users?
Which LLM provider and model version are preferred, and are there cost constraints?
What confidence threshold is acceptable for "proper" answers, and how should low‑confidence cases be handled?
Is there a requirement for multi‑language support?
What data retention period is required, and should backups be encrypted?
Are there any regulatory compliance standards beyond GDPR/CCPA (e.g., HIPAA)?
Should the system provide a UI for file management, or is API‑only sufficient?
